title: Witness & Whistleblower Database
description: The Witness & Whistleblower Database documents individuals who have significantly contributed to the public understanding of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. intelligence officer whose public testimony and written statement raised allegations concerning hidden UAP programs and congressional oversight.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of public congressional testimony, written testimony, public interviews, and documented whistleblower-related reporting.

profiles.status: Former official, public witness, and disclosure advocate
profiles.summary: Former Department of Defense official who testified publicly about alleged secret UAP programs, oversight concerns, and protections for people reporting UAP information.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of public congressional testimony, an official written statement, and testimony concerning whistleblowers and alleged retaliation.

profiles.status: Self-identified federal whistleblower and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Former Air Force geospatial-intelligence specialist who described himself in official written testimony as a federal whistleblower and presented allegations concerning UAP programs and retaliation.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his official House statement explicitly identifies him as a federal whistleblower and records testimony to AARO and the Intelligence Community Inspector General.

profiles.status: Firsthand military witness and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Active-duty U.S. Navy Senior Chief Petty Officer who submitted a firsthand account of a February 2023 UAP event and discussed it publicly in congressional testimony and an interview.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included as a witness profile, not labeled as a whistleblower. His record tests how witness testimony, interviews, claims, organizations, and supporting documents connect.

profiles.status: Former military officer and public disclosure advocate
profiles.summary: Retired U.S. Army colonel, aerospace executive, and public advocate for greater government disclosure concerning UAP.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his former military and national-security background, public statements concerning UAP disclosure, and direct relevance to claims and people already represented in the knowledge graph.

profiles.status: Former government official and public disclosure advocate
profiles.summary: Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and former Senate Intelligence Committee staff director who has advocated for congressional UAP oversight.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his former defense and congressional-intelligence roles, public advocacy for UAP oversight, and involvement in bringing military UAP reporting to congressional attention.

profiles.status: Firsthand military witness and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy F/A-18 pilot, aviation-safety advocate, and congressional witness.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of firsthand military reporting, official congressional testimony, written testimony, and continuing aviation-safety advocacy.

profiles.status: Firsthand military witness and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Retired U.S. Navy commander, firsthand witness to the 2004 Nimitz encounter, and congressional witness.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of firsthand involvement in the 2004 Nimitz encounter, official congressional testimony, written testimony, and documented public interviews.

profiles.status: Former government official and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Former Director of the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office and government witness before Congress.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his leadership of AARO, public and classified congressional testimony, and central role in the official government response to UAP reporting and analysis.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy F/A-18 pilot and firsthand witness to the November 2004 Nimitz encounter.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of her firsthand role in the Nimitz encounter and extensive on-the-record public testimony about the event.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy aviator who recorded the FLIR1 video during the 2004 Nimitz encounter.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he operated the sensor system that produced one of the most significant publicly released videos connected to the Nimitz case.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy radar operator who reported tracking anomalous objects during the 2004 Nimitz events.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his radar account supplies a major sensor-based perspective on the Nimitz timeline.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy technician who has described radar and data-handling activity connected to the 2004 Nimitz events.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his account links the Nimitz case to shipboard radar systems, data handling, and the broader witness network.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Marine Corps aviator who reported observing an unusual disturbance on the ocean during the 2004 Nimitz events.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his observation preceded the best-known visual interception and adds an independent element to the case timeline.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy weapons-systems officer who participated in the 2004 Nimitz interception.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included to represent the full aircrew involved in the visual interception and strengthen the firsthand witness record.

profiles.summary: Former government analyst who publicly associated himself with allegations concerning an alleged program called Immaculate Constellation.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he has publicly identified himself and given extended on-the-record interviews. His central allegations remain unverified publicly.

profiles.summary: Former military servicemember who publicly claimed involvement in recovery operations involving unusual objects.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he has made named, on-the-record claims. The alleged retrieval activities remain unverified publicly.

profiles.summary: Former Defense Intelligence Agency official associated with the AAWSAP program.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his documented government role, authorship, and direct public discussion of AAWSAP and related findings.

profiles.summary: Former intelligence official associated with AAWSAP and leadership of the UAP Task Force.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his established government roles and public statements concerning UAP investigations and institutional handling of the subject.

profiles.status: Military witness and official memorandum author
profiles.summary: Retired U.S. Air Force officer who investigated and documented part of the 1980 Rendlesham Forest incident.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he authored a contemporaneous official memorandum and recorded observations associated with the Rendlesham case.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Air Force security policeman and firsthand witness to the Rendlesham Forest incident.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his contemporaneous written statement is part of the released official record.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Air Force security supervisor who submitted a contemporaneous statement concerning the Rendlesham Forest incident.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his original written statement. Later additions to his account should be distinguished from the contemporaneous record.

profiles.status: Former missile officer and firsthand claimant
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Air Force missile officer who has associated a 1967 missile shutdown with a reported UFO event.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because the missile shutdown is documented and Salas has provided named public testimony. The asserted UFO connection remains disputed.

profiles.status: Former military photographic officer and claimant
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Air Force officer who has claimed that film of a 1964 missile test showed an unidentified object interacting with the test vehicle.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he was a named participant in the missile-test program and has maintained a detailed public account. The interpretation of the film is disputed.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy E-2 Hawkeye crew member associated with recording media and post-event activity following the 2004 Nimitz encounter.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he has publicly described handling E-2 recording media and its removal after the Nimitz events. The account is based on witness interviews rather than a released official chain-of-custody record.

profiles.summary: Former USS Princeton crew member who has publicly described viewing a longer version of video associated with the 2004 Nimitz encounter.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his named public account concerning shipboard video and post-event handling. The longer-video account has not been independently established through a released official copy.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy helicopter crew member connected to post-event activity during the 2004 Nimitz encounter.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his named public account concerning post-event activity associated with the Nimitz case.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy cryptologic technician associated with shipboard records and communications connected to the 2004 Nimitz encounter.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his named public account concerning records, communications, and logs associated with the Nimitz events.

profiles.summary: Astrophysicist who chaired NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA formally appointed him chair of the UAP Independent Study Team and he helped present the team's public findings and recommendations.

profiles.summary: Retired NASA astronaut and former U.S. Navy aviator who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA formally appointed him to the UAP Independent Study Team, adding operational aviation and astronaut experience to the panel.

profiles.summary: Professor and atmospheric-sensing specialist who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA appointed him to provide expertise in atmospheric sensing, remote observation, and data interpretation.

profiles.status: FAA aviation-safety specialist and NASA study-team member
profiles.summary: Federal Aviation Administration specialist who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA appointed him to contribute civil-aviation safety, reporting, and investigative expertise.

profiles.status: FAA policy official and NASA study-team member
profiles.summary: Federal Aviation Administration policy official who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA appointed her to contribute civil-aviation policy and federal-agency coordination experience.

profiles.summary: Computational and data scientist who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA appointed her to provide expertise in complex systems, data analysis, and interdisciplinary research.

profiles.summary: Astrophysicist and data-science specialist who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA appointed her to contribute observational astronomy, survey science, and large-scale data-analysis expertise.

profiles.summary: Astrobiologist and planetary scientist who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA appointed him to contribute planetary-science, astrobiology, and public-science expertise.

profiles.summary: Astronomer and instrumentation specialist who served on NASA's UAP Independent Study Team.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because NASA appointed her to contribute astronomical instrumentation, observational methods, and technosignature-research experience.

profiles.status: Foundational public claimant and disclosure personality
profiles.summary: Public figure whose 1989 interviews alleging work at S-4 near Area 51 profoundly shaped modern UFO culture and reverse-engineering narratives.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his foundational influence on Area 51, S-4, Element 115, and reverse-engineering narratives. His educational and employment claims, evidence, and interpretations remain heavily disputed.

profiles.status: Former Army officer and influential historical claimant
profiles.summary: Retired U.S. Army lieutenant colonel and coauthor of The Day After Roswell, which alleged military transfer and reverse engineering of recovered technology.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his documented Army career and bestselling book made him one of the most influential historical figures in Roswell and reverse-engineering literature. The book's extraordinary claims remain disputed.

profiles.status: Army veteran and historical crash-retrieval claimant
profiles.summary: Retired U.S. Army sergeant who publicly claimed involvement in operations involving recovered craft and non-human beings.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his testimony, books, and public appearances influenced crash-retrieval and military-insider narratives. His extraordinary claims are not independently established.

profiles.status: Former Marine and firsthand crash-encounter claimant
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Marine who publicly described an alleged 1997 encounter with a crashed object in Peru.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his recorded testimony has circulated for decades and is foundational to crash-retrieval research. The event has not been publicly confirmed through official records.

profiles.status: Former Marine and firsthand craft-encounter claimant
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Marine who publicly alleged that he observed a large advanced craft and unidentified personnel during a 2009 humanitarian deployment in Indonesia.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he has made named public statements and reported providing information through whistleblower channels. Major details of the account remain unverified and have been publicly disputed.

profiles.status: Former contractor and South Pole technology claimant
profiles.summary: Former contractor who publicly alleged that South Pole facilities possessed advanced directed-energy and communications capabilities.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he is a prominent modern disclosure personality with a documented interview record. His central South Pole claims are strongly disputed by scientists and fact-checkers.

profiles.summary: Media personality who has publicly claimed involvement in classified medical and biological programs concerning alleged non-human material.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his visibility within modern disclosure media and the influence of his claims on public discussion. Publicly verifiable documentation for the alleged programs is limited.

profiles.status: Rocketry personality and Area 51 technology claimant
profiles.summary: Public speaker and rocketry personality who has long claimed that he viewed an unusual propulsion system at a classified Nevada facility.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his Area 51 account has circulated for decades and influenced reverse-engineering and propulsion narratives. The extraordinary portions of the story are not independently established.

profiles.summary: Constitutional attorney and New Paradigm Institute leader involved in UAP disclosure advocacy and legal support for witnesses and whistleblowers.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his legal work and advocacy connect whistleblowers, disclosure legislation, public-interest law, and the broader UAP transparency movement.

profiles.summary: Stanford pathology professor and Sol Foundation cofounder whose scientific work and public commentary have influenced modern UAP research.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his academic credentials, scientific research, Sol Foundation leadership, and involvement in evidence-focused UAP discussion.

profiles.summary: Harvard astrophysicist, founder of the Galileo Project, and chair of the UAP Science Advisory Council.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he leads instrument-based searches for anomalous aerial and interstellar phenomena and chairs a scientific advisory effort concerning UAP.

profiles.summary: Investigative journalist whose reporting helped bring modern UAP programs, military encounters, and government records into mainstream public discussion.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because her reporting—especially the 2017 New York Times investigation and later reporting on David Grusch—helped shape modern public and congressional awareness.

profiles.summary: Australian investigative journalist and NewsNation correspondent whose interviews have become central records in modern UAP disclosure reporting.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his interviews with David Grusch, Jake Barber, and other sources materially shaped public awareness of modern UAP claims.

profiles.summary: Founder of the Disclosure Project and organizer of the influential 2001 National Press Club witness event.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his witness events, document releases, films, and advocacy strongly influenced public UFO disclosure culture, despite ongoing controversy surrounding some claims and methods.

profiles.status: Former intelligence officer and disclosure organization cofounder
profiles.summary: Former CIA operations officer and cofounder of To The Stars Academy of Arts & Science.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his intelligence background and organizational role connect government experience, Luis Elizondo, Christopher Mellon, public media, and disclosure advocacy.

profiles.summary: Physicist associated with advanced-propulsion studies, AAWSAP-related technical work, and public discussion of government UAP investigations.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his technical publications and government-contract research connect AAWSAP, advanced propulsion, congressional briefings, and scientific discussion of anomalous aerospace phenomena.

profiles.summary: Physicist associated with SRI research, advanced propulsion, EarthTech International, AAWSAP-related work, and To The Stars Academy.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his work and organizational roles connect several major government, private-research, and disclosure initiatives.

profiles.status: Former CIA medical-intelligence officer and researcher
profiles.summary: Physician and former CIA biomedical-intelligence official associated publicly with research into reported physiological effects of anomalous encounters.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his documented intelligence and medical background connect government research, witness-health reports, anomalous effects, and scientific investigation.

profiles.status: Aerospace engineer and former UAP Task Force scientist
profiles.summary: Aerospace engineer, author, television personality, and former chief scientist for the UAP Task Force.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his documented government role and scientific background connect the UAP Task Force, military analysis, media, and Skinwalker Ranch investigations.

profiles.status: Former missile officer and nuclear-facilities witness
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Air Force missile launch officer who has publicly described a 1966 UFO-related event and missile shutdowns near Minot Air Force Base.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because he provides a named military account connecting UFO reports, nuclear command systems, missile shutdowns, and government secrecy.

profiles.status: Former Air Force security policeman and nuclear-facilities witness
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Air Force security policeman who has publicly described a 1977 UFO encounter near a nuclear missile facility.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his named account expands the strategic-facilities witness cluster and connects military security, missile sites, and reported anomalous encounters.

profiles.status: Former Air Force security specialist and strategic-facilities witness
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Air Force security personnel associated with publicly reported 2003 UAP incidents near Vandenberg Air Force Base.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his account helps document a strategic-facilities incident cluster and provides connections to military security, Vandenberg, and other named witnesses.

profiles.status: Former presidential adviser and late-life UAP claimant
profiles.summary: Economist and former presidential adviser whose late-life public statements included extraordinary claims concerning government knowledge of UAP.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his documented government career and the historical significance of his late-life statements. His extraordinary allegations require careful separation from his verified public-service record.
